title: DSDS Example Definitions
description: A unified example model used across DSDS. Each example provides a visual or interactive demonstration via a presentation, a literal value, or both. Optional title, description, and value properties provide context. Presentations are independent typed objects for images, videos, code snippets, and URLs. The `examples` container def adds a `type` discriminator for use as a guideline type in the unified guidelines array, while the `example` def is used for nesting within other guideline items (best-practice examples, variant previews, state previews, interaction illustrations).

presentationImage.description: An image presentation — a screenshot, diagram, annotated mockup, or exported design frame.

type.description: Identifies this presentation as an image.

url.description: URL or relative path to the image file.

alt.description: Alternative text describing the content of the image. MUST be provided for accessibility.

presentationVideo.description: A video presentation — a screen recording, animated transition, or interaction walkthrough.

type.description: Identifies this presentation as a video.

url.description: URL or relative path to the video file.

alt.description: Alternative text describing the content of the video. MUST be provided for accessibility.

presentationCode.description: A code presentation — a source code snippet showing how to use the artifact.

type.description: Identifies this presentation as a code snippet.

code.description: The source code of the example.

presentationUrl.description: A URL presentation — a link to any web resource such as a live demo, interactive example, documentation page, or hosted component story. Use for Storybook stories, CodeSandbox embeds, StackBlitz projects, or any other addressable web content.

type.description: Identifies this presentation as a URL reference to a web resource.

example.description: A single example. Every example requires at least a presentation or a value (or both). The presentation provides a visual or interactive demonstration. The value carries a literal value for API property examples. The optional title and description provide context. When only a value is provided without a presentation, the example represents a concrete data point (e.g., a prop value of 'primary' or a token value of 44) — tools SHOULD render these as table rows or inline displays rather than visual demonstrations.

title.description: A human-readable caption or title for the example. This is a display label (e.g., 'Primary button in default state'). For API property examples, use `value` for the literal content and `title` for the human-readable label.

description.description: An explanation of the example — why it is encouraged, discouraged, or notable.

value.description: A literal value carried by the example. Used for API property examples where the content is a concrete value rather than a visual demonstration. Accepts any JSON type: string (e.g., 'primary'), number (e.g., 44), boolean (e.g., true), object, array, or null. When provided without a presentation, this value alone constitutes the example. Do not use `value` for display text — use `title` for captions and `description` for explanations.

presentation.description: The visual or interactive presentation for the example. Required when `value` is not provided. Optional when `value` is provided — value-only examples represent concrete data points (prop values, token values) that tools render as table rows or inline displays.

examples.description: A guideline container of visual or interactive demonstrations of an artifact. Each item is a single example — a static image, a video recording, a code snippet, a URL to a live demo, or a literal value. Multiple items allow different representations of the same artifact: a static screenshot alongside an interactive Storybook embed, code examples in multiple frameworks, or a sequence of annotated diagrams. This container unifies what was previously split across 'preview' (on components, variants, states) and 'examples' (on patterns) into a single guideline type that can appear on any artifact.

type.description: Identifies this guideline as an examples specification.

items.description: The individual examples. Each item is a single demonstration with a required presentation or value (or both) and optional title and description. The ordering is significant — tools SHOULD display examples in this order. Authors typically place the most representative or hero example first.
